description: Acceptance criteria for the Ralph Wiggum self-correction loop. Each task has pass criteria. The loop runs until all tasks pass or max_iterations is reached. Blocks 1-4 complete.

tasks.name: malware_presence
tasks.description: Confirm presence of STUN.exe on the compromised host
pass_criteria: STUN.exe appears in at least one of: Amcache, Prefetch, MFT, EventLog 4688
Finding includes: tool name, file path, timestamp_utc
Label is CONFIRMED (not INFERRED)
tasks.failure_action: Retry parse_amcache() and parse_prefetch() with filename_filter=['STUN.exe']

tasks.name: execution_evidence
tasks.description: Establish execution evidence for malicious binaries
pass_criteria: At least one binary from IOC list appears in Amcache OR Prefetch
Run timestamp in UTC present
SHA1 hash recorded (Amcache) or run_count recorded (Prefetch)
tasks.failure_action: Retry parse_amcache() then parse_prefetch() — check both sources

tasks.name: persistence_mechanism
tasks.description: Identify how the attacker maintained access across reboots
pass_criteria: At least one persistence artifact found: Run key, service, scheduled task
Artifact source cited: RECmd batch file + registry hive path
Label is CONFIRMED or INFERRED with supporting artifact
tasks.failure_action: Retry parse_registry() with batch_file targeting Run keys and services

tasks.name: lateral_movement
pass_criteria: net.exe or net use command found in EventLog (4688) or Prefetch
UNC path \\172.16.6.12 present in artifact
Timestamp in UTC for the movement event
tasks.failure_action: Retry parse_event_logs(event_ids=[4688]) filtering for net.exe; retry parse_prefetch(filename_filter=['net.exe'])

tasks.name: timeline_integrity
tasks.description: Build a coherent UTC timeline of attacker activity
pass_criteria: Timeline has at least 3 events with UTC timestamps
Events are in chronological order
Each event cites a specific MCP invocation_id
No timestamp conflicts between sources (flag any $SI/$FN mismatches as CONFIRMED timestomping)
tasks.failure_action: parse_mft() with filename_filter on known IOCs to fill timeline gaps

tasks.name: audit_trail
tasks.description: Every finding traces to a logged MCP invocation
pass_criteria: ./audit/mcp.jsonl exists and is non-empty
Every CONFIRMED finding references an invocation_id from mcp.jsonl
No finding cites a source that does not appear in mcp.jsonl
tasks.failure_action: Cross-check report findings against mcp.jsonl entries. Remove any finding without a matching invocation_id.

tasks.name: completion_promise
pass_criteria: Output contains <promise>TASK_COMPLETE</promise> block
confirmed_findings count is >= 1
report path exists on disk
tasks.failure_action: Agent has not completed. Continue investigation.

scoring.description: Used in accuracy report to compare against Protocol SIFT baseline
scoring.note: Each checkpoint scored independently. Partial credit awarded. Follows CFA-Bench methodology.

investigation_stories.name: CRIMSON OSPREY — Initial Compromise
investigation_stories.description: Attacker dropped STUN.exe in System32, masqueraded msedge.exe x7, established C2 to 172.15.1.20

investigation_stories.name: CRIMSON OSPREY — Lateral Movement
investigation_stories.description: Attacker used net.exe PID 9128 to mount H: from 172.16.6.12 R&D subnet

investigation_stories.name: CRIMSON OSPREY — Persistence
investigation_stories.description: pssdnsvc.exe registered as service with name/path mismatch; atmfd.dll absent from filesystem
investigation_stories.entry_point: parse_registry() targeting services and Run keys
